schema_note: Flat array of renewal records. One state can have multiple records (different license_type, cohort, or renewal group). generate.py groups records by state_slug to build one page per state. next_deadline_computed is the actual next upcoming deadline computed from as_of_date forward -- it is NOT a static fact, it must be recomputed if this file is regenerated on a later date. null means the record's pattern is not resolvable to a single date from state-level facts alone (birth-month rules with no personalization, or a firm-cycle anchor year not present in verified source data) -- in those cases see computation/cohort_groups/data_gap_note for how the page should handle it instead of asserting a fake date.
spike_source: b3_saas/day0_spike_results.md -- 10-state Day-0/7 spike, 2026-07-03. CPA-only scope; contractor licensing dropped as structurally fragmented (no single state-level license in 6/10 sampled states).

records.cycle_description: Individual CPA license expires December 31 of the CPA's assigned renewal year, odd or even, assigned at licensure. This is the odd-year cohort: license expires December 31 of odd-numbered years.
Individual CPA license expires December 31 of the CPA's assigned renewal year, odd or even, assigned at licensure. This is the even-year cohort: license expires December 31 of even-numbered years.
Firm licenses expire December 31 of odd-numbered years (a single cohort, simpler than the individual-license split).
Individual CPA license expires September 30, every 3 years. Current cycle ends 2027-09-30.
Firm licenses expire November 30, every 3 years.
records.data_gap_note: Verified spike data gives the recurring month/day (November 30) and the 3-year cycle length but does NOT give a cycle-anchor year for the firm track (unlike the individual track, which has a confirmed 'current cycle ends 2027-09-30'). Rather than guess an anchor year, this field is left null. Do not publish a specific firm-renewal date for Illinois until the anchor year is confirmed against ILGA/IDFPR source.

records.cycle_description: CPA license expires December 31 of odd-numbered years. Current cycle ends 2027-12-31.
Individual CPA license expires December 31 of odd-numbered years. Current cycle ends 2027-12-31.
Firm licenses expire June 30 of even-numbered years. Next: 2028-06-30.

ALL CPA licenses (active, probationary, and CPA-retired) must be renewed, or a change to inactive status requested, by the end of the day on June 30 every year. This applies every year to every licensee — it is not a multi-year cycle. Missing the June 30 cutoff (i.e. failing to act before July 1) triggers a Letter of Demand. Next: 2027-06-30.
records.verification_note: Corrected during sprint-task-5 adversarial verify: initial spike data used 'before July 1' loosely and computed a July 1 deadline; re-checked directly against the NC Board's own 2025-2026 renewal page, which states the cutoff is June 30 (July 1 is when the Letter-of-Demand process starts). Fixed to June 30 with the primary source URL updated accordingly.

records.cycle_description: CPA license expires July 31 of odd-numbered years. Current cycle ends 2027-07-31.

CPA permits expire December 31, with a January 31 grace period, but only about one third of Ohio CPAs are due in any given year due to a rotating 3-group cohort system. Which group a CPA is in determines which December 31 applies to them.
records.grace_period_note: A January 31 grace period follows each December 31 deadline before a permit is considered expired.

records.cycle_description: CPA license expires every 2 years at midnight on the last day of the licensee's own birth month. Licensees with an odd birth year renew in odd expiration years; licensees with an even birth year renew in even expiration years.
Renewal fee is due annually by the last day of the licensee's own birth month.
Mandatory triennial registration (distinct from the license itself) expires in the month prior to the licensee's own birth month, 2-3 years after first registration.

computation.note: Unlike CA/TX, this date cannot be reduced to a lookup table from birth month alone -- it also depends on the individual's first-registration date, which is not a fact this dataset has. The generated page must say so plainly and point the reader to NYSED, not assert a table.
